finding: get_raw served the exact original bytes gated only on files::preview, so a user granted preview but NOT download could obtain the full original file, bypassing a withheld files::download.
stepPage computed currentPage+delta from async React state; two rapid Next clicks read the same stale page so the second is a no-op (double-click Next at page 1 lands on 2 not 3).

controller.destroy() only off()'d eventBus listeners; it never reset the PDFViewer, so a document swap or React StrictMode remount stacked a second PDFViewer (scroll/resize listeners + canvases) over the same non-emptied element -> orphaned canvases + growing memory.
get_raw_docs declared only 200/401/404 but the permission gate returns 403 for a caller lacking the required perm (asserted by pdf_raw_test), leaving 403 undocumented in the OpenAPI operation.
The find match-count ('x of N') had no aria-live region, so a screen-reader user never hears the count update on typing a query or pressing next/prev.
The Ctrl/Cmd+F handler sat on a non-focusable wrapper div; when focus was outside the toolbar/viewer subtree the keydown never reached it and the browser's native find opened instead.
On a document swap the effect set numPages but left currentPage/pageInput/find state at the previous file's values until a pagechanging event fired, briefly showing a stale 'Page N of M'.
Toolbar group dividers were hand-rolled <div class='h-5 w-px bg-border'> instead of the kit Separator component, minor drift from kit-component conventions.
The toolbar disabled-state boundary logic (prev at page 1, next at last page) was inline JSX with no unit coverage, so a regression in those predicates would pass all unit tests.

Verified: the FILE_RAW binary route + fixture live only in the gallery mock-fetch interceptor (dev/gallery), never installed in production; the dev-only fixture is imported solely by mockApi so it tree-shakes out of the app bundle. No exfiltration surface.

Verified: every await is followed by a `cancelled` guard; a load failure sets a settled error state (not an endless spinner) and unmount/fileId-change destroys the loading task, so there is no unhandled rejection or stuck-loading path.

Verified: worker configuration is idempotent (workerConfigured guard) and pdfjs-dist is imported statically ONLY inside pdfjs.ts, which is reached solely via `await import('./pdfjs')`, keeping it in a lazy chunk with no double-init race.

Verified: rendering uses PDF.js's PDFViewer native continuous-scroll virtualization (only visible pages render), and the controller is created once per document (effect keyed on [status,doc,api]), not per render.

Verified: all user-facing strings (aria-labels, tooltips, error copy) match the existing viewer convention (plain English literals, same as body.tsx); no untranslated-vs-translated inconsistency introduced.

FIX_ROUND-1's a11y fix made the scroll container focusable (tabIndex=0) but paired it with focus:outline-none and no replacement, so a keyboard user tabbing into the PDF region got no visible focus indicator (WCAG 2.4.7).
